rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERTO ANTONIO DELGADO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions; Points And Authorites; Declaration Of Richard A. Canatella
rulings.Rulings: The motion for sanctions is denied. Athough there is no legitimate basis for the sanctions request included in the petition, the central issue raised by the petition--the right to the $5000 held by the escrow company on the basis of Objector's demand--is a legitimate, non-frivolous issue, so the petition itself, as opposed to one requested measure of relief, was not brought for an improper purpose or to harass or to delay. Therefore, it was necessary for Objector to respond to the petition on the merits without regard to the sanctions request. In any event, even assuming an award of sanctions could be imposed under CCP section 128.7, the Court would not exercise its discretion to do so on this record. The prayer for sanctions was withdrawn, albeit on the twenty-third day after demand, so it will not have to be litigated on the merits. It is likely motion practice could have been avoided with a little negotiation and patience, so Objector was not sufficiently diligent in trying to resolve the matter. The relatively small amount at issue suggests the amount of sanctions sought by this motion is out of proportion and would be overly punitive. And the Court does not find that the requested sanctions are necessary to deter repetition of the conduct. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by proceeding with the hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Wednesday, June 10, 2015. A previously filed intent to appear by telephone does not constitute the required notice. The prevailing party must submit a form of order consistent with this tentative ruling.
